hypothesis: If the Force Delta (U∆) reflects genuine physical inconsistency in the learned potential energy surface rather than merely statistical noise, then incorporating U∆as a differentiable regularization term during fine-tuning of direct-force pre-trained models on domain-specific data will simultaneously reduce the Force Delta magnitude and improve out-of-distribution generalization accuracy relative to standard fine-tuning, because penalizing internal force disagreement during training will constrain the model to learn more physically consistent representations of the potential energy surface in the target chemical domain.
